session_hint: the Redis cache hit rate drop investigation
question: What was the root cause of the Redis cache hit rate dropping from 94% to 31%?
turns: Cache hit rate on our Redis 7.0 cluster dropped from 94% to 31% overnight. First theory: allkeys-lru eviction policy is kicking out hot keys because maxmemory is too close to capacity.
Checked Redis INFO stats — used_memory is 2.1GB against an 8GB maxmemory limit. The eviction policy hasn't touched anything; evicted_keys is 0. Not an eviction issue, ruling that out.

Second theory: the new product-catalog service deployed yesterday is using the same key prefix and clobbering session keys. Ran SCAN with COUNT 1000 and spot-checked — namespaces are clean, no collisions. Ruling out namespace collision.

Found it: the config push on Tuesday changed session key TTL from 86400 seconds (24h) to 300 seconds (5 minutes) in session_store.yml. Keys expire before most users return, so every request is a cache miss. Reverting TTL to 86400 restored hit rate to 93%. Root cause: TTL misconfigured to 300 seconds (5 minutes).
